key_themes: Apple CEO leadership transition (Tim Cook → John Ternus)
Q1 2026 earnings season peak week (Tesla, Boeing, IBM, GE Vernova)
US-Iran geopolitical tensions weighing on sentiment
Industrial sector rally (SHW, HD, CAT, MMM)
AI infrastructure and enterprise AI spend
Defense sector in focus amid geopolitical risks

stocks.reason: Major breaking news: Tim Cook announced he will step down as CEO effective September 1, 2026, with hardware engineering chief John Ternus named as his successor. Stock slid in extended trading on the surprise announcement.
Reports Q1 2026 earnings on Wednesday April 22; options market implies a ~6% move. Jefferies raised its price target to $350 from $300 ahead of results, expecting ~40% year-on-year earnings growth.
Featured in Monday analyst calls; continued discussion around AI chip demand and Magnificent 7 recovery from earlier-year pullback. Investors buying the dip.
Melius Research doubled down on its Buy rating, raising the two-year price target to $245 from $200 on growing AI server demand and Dell's increasing market share.
Led the industrial sector rally, rising 4.22% to $347.61. Q1 2026 earnings are anticipated with analysts projecting EPS of $2.34, up 4% year-over-year.
Gained 3.55% to $349.45 as part of the broad industrial and home improvement sector rally.

Rose 3.11% to $797.23; Morgan Stanley raised its price target to $430 from $425. Company reported $19.1B in sales and a $51B backlog, expanding into agricultural automation via the Monarch Tractor acquisition.
Gained 3.34% to $155.65 as part of the broad industrial sector rally, benefiting from investor rotation into industrials amid geopolitical uncertainty.

Analyst upgraded to Buy from Hold with a $325 price target (18% upside), citing early price increases to offset energy cost headwinds from US-Iran tensions that could drive second-half earnings upside.
Q1 2026 earnings report due Wednesday April 22; analysts focused on 737 MAX production rate and free cash flow trajectory, with revenue growth forecast exceeding 10% year-over-year.
Earnings due April 22; highlighted as a key beneficiary of data center power demand with multi-year AI infrastructure order visibility driving investor interest.
Earnings due April 22 alongside ServiceNow and Texas Instruments, providing a read on enterprise AI spending. Results closely watched for hybrid cloud and AI revenue trends.
Featured in Monday analyst calls; upgraded to Outperform by Raymond James on April 16. Okta for AI Agents product launches April 30, providing near-term catalyst for stock down ~20% YTD.
Q1 2026 earnings webcast scheduled for April 23; defense sector in focus as renewed US-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz heighten demand for defense spending.

Stock dropped approximately 10% following Q2 revenue guidance miss despite Q1 beat; CEO Reed Hastings departure added to negative sentiment. Remains a key recent market mover discussed heading into the week.
